pi_items.source: Verified in task.json description (line 7), setup_task.sh cleanup (line 12), and export_result.sh (lines 12, 23). This is the exact output path specified.

Verified in task.json description line 2: 'Set the page orientation to **Landscape**.' Also confirmed in export_result.sh which checks for 'style:print-orientation="landscape"' (line 64) and page width > height comparison (lines 69-82).
Verified in task.json description line 4: 'Create a **2-Column** section for the daily itinerary details.' Export script checks for style:columns with fo:column-count >= 2 (lines 94-100).

Task description mentions 3 destination images (rome_colosseum.jpg, florence_duomo.jpg, venice_canal.jpg) but ALSO requires 'The Agency Logo (/home/ga/Documents/images/logo.png)' in the cover section. Setup_task.sh creates exactly 4 image files (lines 103-120). Verifier.py checks for img_count >= 4 for full points (line 71). The metadata value of 3 is incomplete and contradicts the actual requirement of 4.

Verified in setup_task.sh tour_data.json: 'Italian Renaissance Tour' (line 19, tour_title), 'Mr. & Mrs. Sterling' (line 20, client_name), 'Hotel de Russie, Rome' (lines 26, 31, 36), 'Four Seasons Hotel Firenze' (lines 41, 46), 'Hotel Danieli, Venice' (line 51). All 5 expected strings are present in the source data created by setup.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a travel itinerary document in Apache OpenOffice Writer that must be saved as Italy_Proposal_2025.odt in landscape orientation with a 2-column layout. The document requires 4 images total: 3 destination images (rome_colosseum.jpg, florence_duomo.jpg, venice_canal.jpg) plus 1 agency logo (logo.png). The content must include the tour title 'Italian Renaissance Tour', client name 'Mr. & Mrs. Sterling', and three hotel names: 'Hotel de Russie', 'Four Seasons', and 'Hotel Danieli'. All source data is synthetic and created by the setup script, with the tour itinerary data embedded as JSON and images generated programmatically.
